data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`), but it's never added to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't know about it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile>` item in the target after writing the file.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The MSBuild compile item list is already finalized by the time the file is written, so the generated `.cs` file is never passed to the compiler.

The fix is to add a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` item inside the target after writing the file, so the compiler picks it up.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />"}

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `<Compile>` item group. MSBuild's compile list was already finalized, so the generated file was invisible to the C# compiler.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
